prediction: BFSI vertical returns to positive YoY revenue growth within two quarters
asf_basis: ALS 2.05 (Low). LTM's organizational machinery is fast (Obs=2, Dec=2, LV=4). The BFSI decline (-3.9% YoY Q4FY26) is an execution constraint, not a structural incapacity. A fast-adapting organization with a recognized bottleneck and no governance friction should correct a single-vertical execution problem within 6 months.
dimension_driving: Execution Latency (3/5) — execution gap, not permanent impairment
confirming_signal: LTM Q1FY27 earnings discloses BFSI vertical YoY growth ≥ 0%
refuting_signal: BFSI declines further or remains negative in Q1FY27

prediction: LTM FY27 revenue growth rate exceeds FY26 rate (>6% YoY), demonstrating organizational acceleration
asf_basis: ALS 2.05 (Low). Low ALS means the organization can accelerate when commercial execution improves. ASF predicts that a company with ALS < 2.5 should be able to respond to a recognized execution bottleneck within its CRT window (14–23 months). FY27 growth rate above FY26 is the minimum confirmation.
dimension_driving: Observation Latency (2/5) + Decision Latency (2/5) — fast awareness and response
confirming_signal: LTM Q4FY27 full-year revenue growth > 6% YoY
refuting_signal: FY27 growth ≤ 6%, indicating structural constraint ASF did not identify

prediction: BlueVerse outcome-based contract count disclosed publicly and exceeds 10 within 12 months
asf_basis: Decision Latency 2/5 + Learning Velocity 4/5. LTM makes fast product decisions and learns quickly. BlueVerse at 3/100 contracts is an early-stage adoption problem that fast-decision, high-learning organizations typically address within 12 months once the bottleneck is named.
horizon: Any LTM earnings or Investor Day through June 2027
confirming_signal: LTM discloses BlueVerse outcome-based contract count ≥ 10 in any public filing or investor event
refuting_signal: LTM does not disclose BlueVerse contract count by June 2027, or discloses count < 10

prediction: AT&T announces a mandatory AI adoption program with named executive ownership and quarterly adoption targets within two quarters
asf_basis: Decision Latency 4/5. A known problem (58% non-adoption) with no committed structural response is the definition of Decision Latency 4. ASF predicts that sustained high Decision Latency forces an eventual organizational decision. The financial cost of inaction ($250M per 10% adoption lag) creates mounting pressure that reaches a tipping point.
dimension_driving: Decision Latency (4/5) — recognized problem without committed response
confirming_signal: AT&T announces mandatory AI adoption program with VP+ ownership, specific quarterly targets, and manager accountability in any earnings call or press release through December 2026
refuting_signal: No mandatory program announced through Q4 2026 earnings (January 2027)

prediction: AT&T AI savings run-rate fails to reach $2B/year by end of 2026
asf_basis: Execution Latency 5/5 + Dependency Index 5/5. Two maximum-score dimensions. CRT 36.5 months (Critical). The model predicts the $4B target will not be reached in time. The intermediate test is whether savings double from $1B to $2B by year-end 2026. At maximum execution and dependency friction, this requires 4x current adoption velocity in 12 months — which is inconsistent with the measured constraints.
confirming_signal: AT&T Q4 2026 earnings discloses AI savings run-rate below $2B annualized
refuting_signal: AT&T discloses AI savings rate ≥ $2B annualized by Q4 2026

prediction: AT&T $4B AI savings target is not achieved by 2028 (CRT midpoint check)
asf_basis: CRT 36.5 months from June 2026 = mid-2029. The CRT range extends to 45 months = late 2029. The 2030 deadline is technically within range, but any delay increases risk of miss. At the 24-month check (June 2028), AT&T should have achieved approximately $2.5B of $4B if on track. Achieving less confirms CRT trajectory.
dimension_driving: Execution Latency (5/5) + Dependency Index (5/5) + CRT = 36.5mo Critical
confirming_signal: AT&T 2028 annual report discloses AI savings below $3B cumulative
refuting_signal: AT&T achieves $4B savings target before end of 2027

prediction: Boeing production rate does not reach 30 planes/month by Q4 2026
asf_basis: Execution Latency 5/5. CRT 70.7 months (Critical). At maximum execution friction, moving from 18 to 38 planes/month requires 5.9 years at current velocity. Reaching 30 planes in 6 months would require more than doubling the current rate in half a year — inconsistent with a 5/5 execution score and a documented quality governance cycle measured in months.
dimension_driving: Execution Latency (5/5) — production at 47% of target
confirming_signal: Boeing Q4 2026 earnings discloses average monthly production rate below 30 planes

prediction: Boeing management explicitly attributes production rate constraint to quality feedback cycle or governance overhead (not workforce or supply chain alone) in next annual report or earnings call
asf_basis: Execution Latency 5/5 with Feedback Delay 4/5 as the mechanism. ASF identifies the bottleneck as the quality governance feedback cycle — monthly reviews creating multi-week lag between defect occurrence and executive response. If Boeing management confirms this constraint in their own language, the bottleneck identification is validated.
horizon: Boeing Annual Report 2026 (February 2027) or Q3/Q4 2026 earnings call
confirming_signal: Boeing CEO or management team explicitly references quality signal routing, feedback cycle improvement, or real-time quality data as a production rate lever in any public disclosure
refuting_signal: Boeing attributes all production constraints solely to workforce and supplier factors, with no mention of internal quality governance cycle as a constraint

prediction: Boeing does not reach 38 planes/month target before end of 2028
asf_basis: CRT 70.7 months from June 2026 = approximately April 2032. Even the lower bound of the CRT range (52.8 months) places target achievement at October 2030 — after the 2030 competitive window. A 2028 check (30 months into the prediction) should show the program at approximately 40–50% of gap closure if on track. Current rate (18/38) is 47%. By 2028 it should be approximately 26–28 planes/month if CRT is accurate.
confirming_signal: Boeing Q4 2028 earnings discloses production rate below 38 planes/month
refuting_signal: Boeing reaches 38 planes/month by end of 2027

prediction: Both Boeing and AT&T remain in High Risk band (ALS > 3.5) in re-scoring at 12 months (June 2027)
asf_basis: Boeing ALS 3.70, AT&T ALS 3.85. Both in High risk. High ALS reflects structural friction patterns that take 24+ months to materially change. At 12 months, the dimension scores should be within ±0.5 of current values unless a material governance restructuring is announced.
dimension_driving: Both companies: Execution Latency 5/5 unchanged at 12 months
horizon: Re-scoring at June 2027 using Q1 2027 public disclosures
confirming_signal: Re-scoring at June 2027 produces ALS > 3.0 for both companies
refuting_signal: Either company scores ALS < 3.0 at June 2027 re-scoring

prediction: LTM remains in Low Risk band (ALS < 2.5) at 12-month re-scoring
asf_basis: LTM ALS 2.05 (Low). Low ALS reflects organizational agility: fast observation, decision, and learning. These structural capabilities do not degrade rapidly. The 12-month re-scoring should produce ALS < 2.5 unless LTM undergoes a major governance regression.
horizon: Re-scoring at June 2027 using Q4FY27 public disclosures
confirming_signal: Re-scoring at June 2027 produces LTM ALS < 2.5

prediction: CRT risk band classification is correct for ≥ 75% of the 20 historical programs in the calibration study when re-evaluated with revised T_base values
asf_basis: Calibration study found 14/20 programs within ±30% with current T_base. With revised T_base values (+25% correction factor), directional accuracy should improve. This prediction tests whether the calibration produces a measurable improvement in risk band accuracy.
dimension_driving: CRT T_base recalibration: Manufacturing 7.8, Telecom 6.5, Technology 7.5, FinServ 5.6, Gov 7.7
horizon: CRT re-calculation with revised T_base (can be done immediately — internal)
confirming_signal: Re-running calibration study with recommended T_base values produces ≥ 15/20 programs within ±30% and ≥ 15/20 correct risk band classification
refuting_signal: Revised T_base values produce fewer than 12/20 programs within ±30%

prediction: External analyst study produces Cohen's Kappa ≥ 0.50 and ≥ 80% bottleneck agreement across 5 companies
asf_basis: Simulated IRR produced κ = 0.739 with 100% bottleneck agreement. External human analysts will produce lower agreement than the simulation because they bring diverse prior knowledge, different reading emphases, and genuine uncertainty. Setting κ ≥ 0.50 (moderate agreement) as the prediction is more conservative than the simulated result and accounts for real-world analyst variance.
dimension_driving: IRR simulation: three contested Boeing dimensions suggest Boeing is the hardest company; LTM and AT&T showed 100% exact agreement
horizon: External validation study completion (6–8 weeks from recruitment)
confirming_signal: External study with 3 analysts and 5 companies produces mean κ ≥ 0.50 across all analyst pairs, and ≥ 80% (12/15) analyst-pair-company combinations agree on primary bottleneck
refuting_signal: External study produces κ < 0.40 or bottleneck agreement < 70%
